\"synthesize_total_ms\",\n232\t    \"escalate_ms\",\n233\t    \"sse_emit_ms\",\n234\t    \"stt_ms\",\n235\t    \"first_render_ms\",\n236\t)\n237\t\n238\t# Valid intent status values (plan §10 The §10 The Async Path: Visible Aging)\n242\t# task-profile: 6h (async bead-backed tasks)\n243\t# hot-path intents: 30s (budget is 3s, flag at 10x for aged pending)\n244\tDEFAULT_SLA_HOURS: dict[str, seconds for hot-path action intents\n248\t    \"lookup\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path lookup intents\n249\t    \"brainstorm\": 0.5,   # 30 minutes for brainstorm (may need user iteration)\n250\t    \"reminder\": 24.0,    # 24 hours for reminders\n251\t}\n252\t\n253\t# Circuit breaker thresholds (plan §10 The Async Path)\n254\tCIRCUIT_BREAKER_REFUSAL_THRESHOLD = 3  # Fence after N refusals\n255\tCIRCUIT_BREAKER_AGE_THRESHOLD_HOURS = 24.0  # Fence after N hours without progress\n256\t\n257\t\n258\tclass SessionStore:\n259\t    \"\"\"Session store with WAL mode for concurrent access.\"\"\"\n260\t\n261\t    def __init__(self, db_path: Path):\n262\t        self.db_path = db_path\n263\t\n264\t    async def initialize(self) -> None:\n265\t        \"\"\"Initialize database with schema and WAL mode.\"\"\"\n266\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n267\t            # Enable WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes\n268\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n269\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n270\t\n271\t            # Create schema\n272\t            await db.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\n273\t            await db.commit()\n274\t\n275\t            # Additive migrations: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS above creates new\n276\t            # columns only for freshly-made DBs; an existing data/session.db\n277\t            # (e.g. the live Hetzner box) keeps its old column set and must be\n278\t            # ALTERed. Each statement is guarded by a PRAGMA table_info probe so\n279\t            # it is idempotent and never re-runs once the column exists.\n280\t            await self._migrate_additive_columns(db)\n281\t\n282\t    @staticmethod\n283\t    async def _migrate_additive_columns(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n284\t        \"\"\"Idempotently add columns introduced after the initial schema.\"\"\"\n285\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(results)\") as cur:\n286\t            result_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n287\t        if \"result_type\" not in result_cols:\n288\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN result_type TEXT\")\n289\t        if \"card_fallback\" not in result_cols:\n290\t            await db.execute(\n291\t                \"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0\"\n292\t            )\n293\t\n294\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n295\t            intent_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n296\t        if \"lookup_kind\" not in intent_cols:\n297\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents ADD COLUMN lookup_kind TEXT\")\n298\t\n299\t        # Migrate intents.status CHECK constraint to include 'stuck' and 'failed'\n300\t        # SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we need to recreate the table\n301\t        # First, check if the table still has the old constraint (without stuck/failed)\n302\t        await SessionStore._migrate_intents_status_enum(db)\n303\t\n304\t        # Migrate results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\n305\t        await SessionStore._migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db)\n306\t\n307\t        # Migrate bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1\n308\t        await SessionStore._migrate_bead_watch_comment_high_water(db)\n309\t\n310\t        await db.commit()\n311\t\n312\t    @staticmethod\n313\t    async def _migrate_intents_status_enum(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n314\t        \"\"\"Migrate intents table to support 'stuck' and 'failed' status values.\n315\t\n316\t        Plan §10 The Async Path: extend intents status enum with stuck/failed.\n317\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we recreate the table.\n318\t        \"\"\"\n319\t        # Check if migration is needed by looking for 'stuck' in the schema\n320\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n321\t            cols = await cur.fetchall()\n322\t            status_col = None\n323\t            for col in cols:\n324\t                if col[1] == \"status\":\n325\t                    status_col = col\n326\t                    break\n327\t\n328\t        # If no status column or already has stuck/failed, skip migration\n329\t        if not status_col:\n330\t            return\n331\t\n332\t        # Check if the CHECK constraint already includes stuck/failed\n333\t        # by inspecting the SQL for the intents table\n334\t        async with db.execute(\n335\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='intents'\"\n336\t        ) as cur:\n337\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n338\t            if table_sql and (\"'stuck'\" in table_sql[0] or \"'failed'\" in table_sql[0]):\n339\t                # Already migrated\n340\t                return\n341\t\n342\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with new constraint\n343\t        logger.info(\"Migrating intents.status to include 'stuck' and 'failed' values\")\n344\t\n345\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n346\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n347\t\n348\t        try:\n349\t            # Create new table with updated constraint\n350\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n351\t                CREATE TABLE intents_new (\n352\t                    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n353\t                    utterance_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n354\t                    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n355\t                    topic_id     TEXT,\n356\t                    project_slug TEXT,\n357\t                    intent_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n358\t                    lookup_kind  TEXT,\n359\t                    status       TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')) DEFAULT 'pending',\n360\t                    bead_ref     TEXT,\n361\t                    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n362\t                    resolved_at  INTEGER,\n363\t                    FOREIGN KEY (utterance_id) REFERENCES utterances(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n364\t                    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n365\t                    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n366\t                )\n367\t            \"\"\")\n368\t\n369\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n370\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n371\t                INSERT INTO intents_new\n372\t                SELECT id, utterance_id, session_id, topic_id, project_slug,\n373\t                       intent_type, lookup_kind, status, bead_ref, created_at, resolved_at\n374\t                FROM intents\n375\t            \"\"\")\n376\t\n377\t            # Recreate indexes\n378\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_session\")\n379\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_topic\")\n380\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_status\")\n381\t\n382\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_session ON intents_new(session_id)\")\n383\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_topic ON intents_new(topic_id)\")\n384\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_status ON intents_new(status)\")\n385\t\n386\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n387\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE intents\")\n388\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents_new RENAME TO intents\")\n389\t\n390\t            await db.commit()\n391\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: intents.status now supports 'stuck' and 'failed'\")\n392\t\n393\t        except Exception as e:\n394\t            await db.rollback()\n395\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate intents.status: {e}\")\n396\t            raise\n397\t\n398\t    @staticmethod\n399\t    async def _migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n400\t        \"\"\"Migrate results table to allow NULL intent_id for monitoring-originated results.\n401\t\n402\t        Plan §10 Bead Watcher: monitoring-originated results have intent_id=NULL.\n403\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN to drop NOT NULL, so we recreate the table.\n404\t        \"\"\"\n405\t        # Check if migration is needed by inspecting the results table schema\n406\t        async with db.execute(\n407\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='results'\"\n408\t        ) as cur:\n409\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n410\t            if not table_sql:\n411\t                return  # Already migrated\n417\t                    return\n418\t\n419\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with nullable intent_id\n420\t        logger.info(\"Migrating results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\")\n421\t\n422\t        # Copy data from old table to new table\n452\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n453\t                INSERT INTO results_new\n454\t                SELECT * FROM results\n455\t            \"\"\")\n456\t\n457\t            # With DEFAULT -1, no comments are skipped\n485\t        initially, and only comments with index > comment_high_water are processed.\n486\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN DEFAULT, so we recreate the table.\n487\t        \"\"\"\n488\t        # Check if migration is needed by inspecting the bead_watch table schema\n489\t        async with db.execute(\n490\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='bead_watch'\"\n491\t        ) as cur:\n492\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n493\t            if not table_sql:\n494\t                return  # Table doesn't exist yet\n495\t\n496\t            # If comment_high_water already has DEFAULT -1, skip migration\n497\t            if \"DEFAULT -1\" in table_sql[0]:\n498\t                # Already migrated\n499\t                return\n500\t\n501\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with new default\n502\t        logger.info(\"Migrating bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1\")\n503\t\n504\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n505\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n506\t\n507\t        try:\n508\t            # aiosqlite uses connection-per-context, so just ensure checkpoint\n552\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n553\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)\")\n554\t\n555\t    # one transaction.\n606\t\n607\t        The topics table references sessions with ``ON DELETE SET NULL``, so\n608\t        topics are *not* cascade-removed even if FKs were on — deleting them\n609\t        explicitly is required for a true clean teardown.\n610\t\n611\t        Returns ``{\"session_removed\": 0|1, \"topics_removed\": <int>}``.\n612\t        \"\"\"\n613\t        async with session_removed = 1 if await cur.fetchone() else 0\n625\t\n626\t            # Children first, parents last (matches FK dependency order).\n627\t            await db.execute(\n628\t Ensure the row exists (created_at set once, on first write).\n1241\t            await db.execute(\n1242\t                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO dispatch_timings (intent_id, created_at) VALUES (?, ?)\",\n1243\t                (intent_id, now),\n1244\t            )\n1245\t            if cols:\n1246\t                set_clause = \", \".join(f\"{c} = ?\" for c in cols)\n1247\t                params = [*cols.values(), intent_id]\n1248\t                await db.execute(\n1249\t                    f\"UPDATE dispatch_timings SET {set_clause} WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n1250\t                    params,\n1251\t                )\n1252\t            await db.commit()\n1253\t\n1254\t    async def get_dispatch_timings(self, intent_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n1255\t        \"\"\"Get the dispatch_timings row for one intent thread, or None.\"\"\"\n1256\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1257\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n1258\t            async with db.execute(\n1259\t                \"SELECT * FROM dispatch_timings WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n1260\t                (intent_id,),\n1261\t            ) as cursor:\n1262\t                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n1263\t                return dict(row) if row else None\n1264\t\n1265\t    async def get_latency_percentiles(self, since: int | None = None) -> dict:\n1266\t        \"\"\"Aggregate p50/p95 per stage across dispatch_timings rows.\n1267\t\n1268\t        Returns ``{stage: {\"p50\": ms, \"p95\": ms, \"count\": n}}`` for every stage\n1269\t        that has at least one non-NULL value. NULLs are skipped per stage (so a\n1270\t        stage not yet captured for any dispatch simply doesn't appear). Pass\n1271\t        ``since`` (a unix timestamp) to window to recent dispatches only — the\n1272\t        latency-baseline bead consumes the un-windowed call to fill the plan's\n1273\t        global Measured p50/p95 columns.\n1274\t\n1275\t        Percentiles are nearest-rank (ceil(q/100 * n)), computed in Python\n1276\t        because SQLite has no built-in percentile.\n1277\t        \"\"\"\n1278\t        from ..instrument.timings import percentiles\n1279\t\n1280\t        result: dict[str, dict] = {}\n1281\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1282\t            for stage in DISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS:\n1283\t                if since is not None:\n1284\t                    query = (\n1285\t                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n1286\t                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL AND created_at >= ? \"\n1287\t                        f\"ORDER BY {stage}\"\n1288\t                    )\n1289\t                    params: tuple = (since,)\n1290\t                else:\n1291\t                    query = (\n1292\t                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n1293\t                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL ORDER BY {stage}\"\n1294\t                    )\n1295\t                    params = ()\n1296\t                async with db.execute(query, params) as cursor:\n1297\t                    values = [row[0] for row in await cursor.fetchall()]\n1298\t                if not values:\n1299\t                    continue\n1300\t                pct = percentiles(values, qs=(50, 95))\n1301\t                result[stage] = {\n1302\t                    \"p50\": pct[50],\n1303\t                    \"p95\": pct[95],\n1304\t                    \"count\": len(values),\n1305\t                }\n1306\t        return result\n1307\t\n1308\t    async def get_session_feedback_summary(\n1309\t        self,\n1310\t        session_id: str,\n1311\t        since: int | None = None,\n1312\t    ) -> dict:\n1313\t        \"\"\"Get a summary of feedback signals for a session.\"\"\"\n1314\t        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n1315\t\n1316\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1317\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n1318\t\n1319\t            # Get signal counts by type\n1320\t            since_clause = since or one_hour_ago\n1321\t            async with db.execute(\n1322\t                \"\"\"SELECT signal_type, COUNT(*) as count\n1323\t                   FROM feedback_signals\n1324\t                   WHERE session_id = ? AND timestamp >= ?\n1325\t                   GROUP BY signal_type\"\"\",\n1326\t                (session_id, since_clause)\n1327\t            ) as cursor:\n1328\t                type_counts = {row[\"signal_type\"]: row[\"count\"] for row in await cursor.fetchall()}\n1329\t\n1330\t            # Get average ack delay\n1331\t            async with db.execute(\n1332\t                \"\"\"SELECT AVG(CAST(data->>'ack_delay_seconds' AS INTEGER)) as avg_delay\n1333\t                   FROM feedback_signals\n1334\t                   WHERE session_id = ? \"avg_ack_delay_seconds\": avg_ack_delay,\n1343\t            }\n1344\t\n1345\t    async def create_result_with_diff(\n1346\t        self,\n1347\t        intent_id: str,\n1348\t        topic_id: str,\n1349\t        session_id: str,\n1350\t        summary: str,\n1351\t        data: dict,\n1352\t        urgency: str = \"normal\",\n1353\t    ) -> tuple[str, bool]:\n1354\t        \"\"\"\n1355\t        Create a new result with automatic diff computation.\n1356\t\n1357\t        Returns (result_id, has_diff).\n1358\t\n1359\t        Convenience method that automatically computes the diff against\n1360\t        the previous result for the same topic (if one exists).\n1361\t        \"\"\"\n1362\t        from ..diff.engine import get_diff_engine\n1363\t\n1364\t        # Get previous None\n1369\t        diff_data = None\n1370\t        has_diff = False\n1371\t\n1372\t        if previous_result:\n1373\t            diff_engine = get_diff_engine()\n1374\t            diff_result =     )\n1379\t\n1380\t            if diff_result.has_changes:\n1381\t                has_diff = True\n1382\t                diff_summary = diff_result.change_summary\n1383\t                diff_data =       for f in diff_result.fields\n1392\t                    ],\n1393\t                    \"summary\": diff_result.summary,\n1394\t                }\n1395\t\n1396\t        # Create the result with
